title: Class 0 — random_position (non-puzzle)
caption: Quiet endgame, Black to move.  Best move \texttt{Nc4+} wins material but the position came from random midgame sampling rather than a curated puzzle source.

title: Class 1 — verified-near-puzzle (hard negative)
caption: Looks like a puzzle (queen \& bishop coordinated against the king), but Stockfish verifies \textbf{pv\_gap = 27 cp}: the second-best move scores almost identically, so there is no unique winning idea.  This is the hard-negative class that the scout was built to distinguish.

title: Class 2 — puzzle\_filter\_matched (true puzzle)
caption: Black to move.  \texttt{Qxc7} is the unique winning move (\textbf{pv\_gap = 838 cp}); all other moves either lose material or fail to convert.  Tactical motif, single-solution: this is what the binary head must recognise.
